title: Numeric Functions
content:
### Numeric Functions

SQL provides a range of functions for mathematical operations and numeric calculations.

---

### Rounding Functions

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| ROUND(value, decimals) | Rounds to specified decimal places | ROUND(3.14159, 2) => 3.14 |
| CEIL(value) | Rounds up to nearest integer | CEIL(3.2) => 4 |
| FLOOR(value) | Rounds down to nearest integer | FLOOR(3.8) => 3 |
| TRUNCATE(value, decimals) | Truncates without rounding | TRUNCATE(3.14159, 2) => 3.14 |

### Mathematical Functions

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| ABS(value) | Absolute value | ABS(-15) => 15 |
| MOD(a, b) | Remainder of a / b | MOD(10, 3) => 1 |
| POWER(a, b) | a raised to power b | POWER(2, 3) => 8 |
| SQRT(value) | Square root | SQRT(25) => 5 |
| EXP(value) | e raised to power value | EXP(1) => 2.71828 |
| LN(value) | Natural logarithm | LN(2.71828) => 1 |
| LOG(base, value) | Logarithm with base | LOG(10, 100) => 2 |

### Trigonometric Functions

```sql
SELECT SIN(0), COS(0), TAN(0), PI();
-- Result: 0, 1, 0, 3.14159
```

### Sign and Rounding

```sql
SELECT SIGN(-50),  -- -1
       SIGN(0),    -- 0
       SIGN(50);   -- 1

SELECT RAND();  -- Random number between 0 and 1
```

> **Key Point:** The ROUND function rounds to the nearest value, while TRUNCATE simply discards extra decimal places without rounding.
codeExample:
SELECT product_name,
       price,
       ROUND(price * 0.08, 2) AS tax_amount,
       ROUND(price * 1.08, 2) AS total_with_tax,
       CEIL(price) AS ceiling_price,
       FLOOR(price) AS floor_price
FROM products;

quiz.question: What is the difference between ROUND and TRUNCATE?
options: ROUND rounds, TRUNCATE discards without rounding
